items.summary: Fix the backend owner role validation logic in backend/main.mo so that the authenticated Internet Identity principal stored as the owner at initialization is correctly compared against the caller principal in all protected functions (addWorker, addHoliday, markAttendance, and any other owner-gated calls). The comparison must use structural principal equality, not string coercion or any other indirect method that could silently fail.

Audit and fix the owner initialization flow in backend/main.mo to ensure the owner principal is correctly recorded when the canister is first set up. If the owner principal is stored via a setup/register call, verify that it is written to stable storage and survives canister upgrades. If it was never written (null/unset), add a safe re-initialization path that allows the current authenticated caller to claim ownership when no owner is set.

Verify that the frontend actor hook (useActor.ts / useQueries.ts) always constructs the backend actor with the authenticated Internet Identity agent, so that msg.caller on the backend receives the real owner principal and not the anonymous principal. If any mutation call (addWorker, addHoliday, attendance mark) is invoked before the actor is fully initialized with the identity, add a guard that waits for actor readiness before dispatching the call.
